note_text: Pt presents with 3 day history of pleuritic chest pain, shortness of breath and haemoptysis.
68 year old female with sudden onset shortness of breath and pleuritic chest pain. History of previous DVT. Returned from long haul flight 5 days ago. Calf swelling noted on right leg.
55 year old male on active cancer treatment presents with collapse and haemoptysis. Severe pleuritic chest pain. Previous PE two years ago. Patient hypotensive and tachycardic.

32 year old male presenting for routine follow-up. Denies chest pain. No shortness of breath. No haemoptysis. No leg swelling or calf tenderness. No recent surgery or immobilisation. No family history of DVT or PE.
44 year old female on the oral contraceptive pill presents with new onset shortness of breath and faint episode. No cough. No leg symptoms reported.
71 year old male with recent surgery 2 weeks ago presents with pleuritic chest pain and shortness of breath. Tachypnoeic on assessment. Calf tenderness noted on left. Family history of DVT.
Patient with history of PE 3 years ago presents with dizzy spells and mild chest pain. Denies haemoptysis. No leg swelling.
28 year old female presenting with sore throat and mild cough for 3 days. No chest pain. No shortness of breath. No haemoptysis. No leg symptoms. No risk factors for PE.
60 year old female presenting with coughing up blood and shortness of breath. Currently on chemotherapy for breast cancer. No prior DVT or PE. Oxygen saturations low.
35 year old male with recent long haul flight returning from Australia. Presents with leg swelling and mild shortness of breath. No chest pain. No haemoptysis. No prior history of DVT.
